fields.name: Career & Financial Independence
fields.description:
Welcome to Career & Financial Independence — Let's Build Our Future Together


Whether you're just starting out, switching careers, navigating the gender pay gap, or working towards financial freedom — this is your space.
Too often, women are discouraged from talking openly about money, ambition, and career goals. Here, we do the opposite. This community exists so you can ask the questions you were told not to ask, share wins without apology, and get real advice from women who've been there.
Here's what this space is for:

Negotiating your salary and knowing your worth
Building savings, investments, and financial security
Career pivots, promotions, and navigating workplace challenges
Side hustles, freelancing, and entrepreneurship
Practical budgeting and money management tips

No question is too basic. No goal is too big.
Drop a comment and introduce yourself — where are you in your career journey right now?

fields.name: Conditioning & Marriage Pressure
fields.description:
"You weren't born wanting to get married. You were taught to."
From the time you could understand language, you were being prepared.
Fairy tales ended at the wedding. Relatives asked "when will you settle down?" before you'd even figured out who you were. Mothers planned futures for daughters that started and ended with a husband. Aunties measured your worth in terms of your "marriageability."
Nobody asked you what you wanted. They assumed they already knew.
This is conditioning — the quiet, constant process of teaching a person to want what society needs them to want. And when it comes to marriage, women especially are conditioned deeply and early.
You're told marriage is the goal, not an option.
You're told being unmarried past a certain age means failure.
You're told your needs, your career, your ambitions are secondary — things to manage around a marriage, not build a life around.
And the cruelest part? Most of us don't even notice it happening. By the time the pressure arrives — from family, from culture, from your own inner voice — it feels like your own desire.
That's how good the conditioning is.
This series is about noticing it. Naming it. And reclaiming the choice that was quietly taken from you.
Because you deserve to decide about marriage from a place of genuine wanting — not fear, not guilt, not a deadline someone else set for your life.
Follow along. This conversation is long overdue.

Save this if it resonates. Share it with someone who needs to hear it.

fields.name: Courage Stories (Small Wins Matter)
fields.description:
Nobody talks about the small kind of courage.
Not the kind that makes headlines.
Not the kind that gets applause.
The kind that happens quietly — when no one is watching.
Like texting back after weeks of silence because you finally found the words.
Like saying "I'm not okay" to someone you usually pretend to be fine around.
Like going to that class alone, even though every part of you wanted an excuse to skip.
Like choosing yourself — just once — instead of shrinking to keep the peace.
That's courage too.
We've been sold this idea that bravery looks big. That it roars. That it arrives perfectly dressed, certain of itself, ready for the moment.
But most real courage? It whispers. It trembles. It shows up with shaking hands and does the thing anyway.
The conversation you finally had.
The boundary you held even when it felt selfish.
The dream you admitted out loud for the first time.
The day you decided your comfort mattered.
These are not small things.
These are the moments that quietly change the shape of your life.
This series is for those moments. The ones you almost didn't count. The wins you dismissed with "it's not a big deal."
It is a big deal.
You are a big deal.
Welcome to Courage Stories — where small wins get the recognition they always deserved.
Drop a 🔥 if you've had a small-courage moment this week. I want to hear it.

Save this for the days you forget that showing up counts.

fields.name: Health & Self Care
fields.description:
Self care isn't a spa day. It's the hard, unglamorous work of keeping yourself alive.
Not the candles.
Not the face mask.
Not the aesthetically pleasing smoothie bowl.
Real self care looks like this —
Drinking water before you reach for your phone.
Sleeping even when your to-do list isn't done.
Saying no to something that drains you, without explaining yourself.
Going to the doctor you've been avoiding for six months.
Eating a real meal instead of surviving on chai and anxiety.
Taking your medicine. Consistently. Without guilt.
That's the self care nobody posts about.
Because it doesn't look pretty. It looks like discipline on bad days. It looks like choosing yourself when you're exhausted and every other option feels easier.
We live in bodies that carry us through everything — every heartbreak, every deadline, every sleepless night, every version of ourselves we've ever been.
And most of us treat our bodies like an afterthought.
Health isn't just the absence of illness. It's the daily, quiet act of showing up for yourself — before you show up for anyone else.
You cannot pour from empty. You cannot show up fully when you're running on fumes and ignoring every signal your body sends you.
This series is about coming back to yourself.
Not perfectly. Not all at once.
Just one small, intentional act at a time.
Because you deserve the same care you so freely give to everyone else.
What's one small thing you did for your health today? Tell me below. 👇

Save this as your reminder. Your health is not a luxury — it's the foundation of everything.

fields.name: Identity & Self-Discovery
fields.description:
At some point, you stopped asking yourself what you wanted. You just started becoming what everyone needed.
Think about it.
The daughter who was always responsible.
The friend who was always available.
The girl who was always agreeable.
The woman who made herself smaller so others could feel bigger.
Role after role after role.
And somewhere in the middle of all that performing — you lost the thread back to yourself.
You don't even know when it happened. There was no single moment. Just a slow, quiet drift. Years of "I'm fine." Years of adjusting. Years of becoming so good at reading what others needed that you forgot to ask what you need.
And now sometimes you sit alone and wonder — who am I, actually? Outside of what I do for people? Outside of what they expect from me?
That question isn't a crisis.
That question is the beginning.
Identity isn't something you find once and keep forever. It's something you return to. Something you unlearn and rebuild. Something that asks you — again and again — to be honest about what fits and what never really did.
Who are you when no one is watching?
What do you love that has nothing to do with being useful?
What have you been carrying that was never yours to begin with?
This series is about those questions.
Not to overwhelm you — but to walk back to yourself, one honest conversation at a time.
Because the most important relationship you will ever have is the one you have with yourself.
And it's never too late to start getting to know her.
What's one thing you know to be deeply, truly yours — a value, a dream, a way of being? Share below. 👇

Save this if you've ever felt like a stranger to yourself. You're not lost — you're just finding your way back.

fields.name: Pregnancy and Motherhood
fields.description:
“Ek Nayi Zindagi… Aur Ek Nayi Main”

Pregnancy sirf ek phase nahi hota…
ye ek poori duniya badalne ka ehsaas hota hai।

Jab pehli baar pata chalta hai ki tumhare andar ek chhoti si jaan hai,
toh darr bhi hota hai… aur ek ajeeb si khushi bhi।

Kabhi bina wajah aansu aate hain,
kabhi chhoti si baat pe hasi rukti nahi…
hormones bolte hain log—
par sach toh ye hai ki dil dheere dheere maa ban raha hota hai।

Raat ko neend kam aati hai,
par sapne zyada aate hain…
us chhoti si muskaan ke,
jo abhi duniya mein aayi bhi nahi।

Aur phir ek din…
jab tum usse apni baahon mein pakadti ho,
tab samajh aata hai—
tum sirf ek baby ko janam nahi deti…
tum ek nayi khud ko janam deti ho।

Motherhood perfect nahi hota,
par sach mein…
ye sabse khoobsurat journey hoti hai ❤️

fields.name: Divorce & New Beginnings
fields.description:
“Starting Over Isn’t Starting From Zero”

Divorce is not just the end of a relationship.
It’s the end of plans, habits, and a life you once thought was permanent.

It can feel like everything has fallen apart—
like you’re standing in the middle of a life you don’t recognize anymore.

There will be quiet moments that feel too loud,
and memories that show up uninvited.

But starting over doesn’t mean you’ve lost everything.
It means you’re finally letting go of what wasn’t working.

You carry forward your strength, your lessons, and your courage.
That’s not nothing—that’s your foundation.

Some days will be heavy.
Some days will feel hopeful.

Both matter.

Because little by little, you begin again—
not as the person you were before,
but as someone stronger, wiser, and more aware of what you truly deserve.

Divorce closed one chapter.
But it also gave you something powerful—
a chance to write the next one your way.

fields.name: Community Talk
fields.description:
Welcome to Community Talk — This Is Your Space to Be Heard

This is the heart of WomenKonnect.
Community Talk is where we come together — not just to talk about serious topics, but to simply connect as women. Share what's on your mind, celebrate small wins, vent about a tough day, or just say hello.
This space is for:

Everyday conversations and real talk
Sharing experiences that don't fit anywhere else
Finding your people and building friendships
Asking for advice — big or small
Uplifting each other just because

There are no wrong topics here. If it matters to you, it matters here.
To get us started — introduce yourself! Tell us your name, where you're from, and one thing that made you smile this week. We'd love to meet you. 💬

fields.title: Career mein aage jaana hai — par pehle ghar bhi toh sambhalna hai.
fields.content:
Ek din ka scene imagine karo. Subah uthke tiffin banao, school drop karo, office pohoncho, back-to-back meetings survive karo, shaam ko ghar aao — aur tab shuru hoti hai doosri shift. Khana. Homework. Laundry. Dishes. Aur yeh sab karne ke baad, jab sab so jaate hain — tab kahin aap apne career ke baare mein sochti hain. Woh course jo complete nahi hua. Woh promotion jo maangni thi. Woh idea jo dimaag mein hai par kabhi bahar nahi aaya.

Yeh sirf aapki story nahi hai. Yeh karoron women ki story hai.

"Hamara rasta sirf lamba nahi hai — hamara rasta double shift wala hai. Aur phir bhi hum chal rahi hain."
Society ne yeh ek silent agreement bana li hai ki ghar ki zimmedaari by default woman ki hai. Aur career? Woh "extra" hai. Luxury hai. Jaise ambition koi selfishness ho. Jaise apne liye kuch chahna, ghar ke baaki logo se kuch "chheenna" ho.

Par yeh sach nahi hai. Aur hume yeh bolna band karna hoga khud ko.

The real picture
Studies kehti hain ki working women ghar pe average 4–5 ghante unpaid domestic work karti hain daily — apne male counterparts ke comparison mein jo karte hain roughly 1–2 ghante. Career mein same starting point se bhi, hum ek invisible weight ke saath daud rahi hain. Aur phir log poochte hain — "aage kyun nahi badhi abhi tak?"

Toh ab sawaal yeh nahi hai ki "career kaise grow karein" — sawaal yeh hai ki itne sab ke beech mein, apne liye jagah kaise nikaalein.

Kuch cheezein jo actually kaam karti hain —

1.  Apna time publicly protect karo Agar aap 8–9 pm study ya work karna chahti hain, toh woh block calendar pe daalein — aur ghar mein bhi bolo. "Yeh mera time hai." Yeh selfish nahi, yeh survival hai.

2. Ghar ki zimmedaari redistribute karo — negotiate karo Partner hai toh conversation karo. Bacche bade hain toh unhe involve karo. Yeh unki bhi zimmedaari hai. Hum akele sab karne ki "superhero" image ko thoda retire karna hoga.

3. Progress ko micro mein dekhna seekho Bade goals overwhelm karte hain jab time kam ho. Ek choti cheez — ek course module, ek email, ek connection — kafi hai. Compounding kaam karta hai careers mein bhi.

4. Apni wins track karo — even the tiny ones Ek "brag doc" banao. Aaj aapne kya kiya jo worth noting hai? Jab aap exhausted hoti hain aur lagta hai "kuch nahi hua" — yeh doc aapko yaad dilaata hai ki kitna ho raha hai.

5. Guilt ko data mat banne do. Guilt ek feeling hai — fact nahi. Yeh feeling aapko nahi batati ki aap buri maa hain, buri behen hain, buri bahu hain. Yeh sirf batati hai ki aapko bahut zyada expect kiya gaya hai.

Career growth sirf promotions aur salaries nahi hai. Kabhi kabhi growth yeh hai ki aap ek din apne liye 30 minutes nikaalpaayi — bina guilt ke. Woh bhi progress hai. Woh bhi count karta hai.

Aur agar aaj woh bhi nahi ho paya — kal hoga. Aap jaari hain. Yeh kaafi hai.

"Tumhara rasta lamba zaroor hai — par tum akeli nahi chal rahi."

fields.title: Community Talk Yahan koi performance nahi — bas aapke aur mere jaise sab...
fields.content:
Ek jagah jahan "I'm fine" bolne ki zaroorat nahi.

Kabhi aisa hua hai ki kuch feel hua — khushi, frustration, confusion, ya sirf ek weird lonely feeling — aur samajh nahi aaya kise bolein? Family samjhegi nahi. Friends busy hain. Partner ke saath explain karna bahut energy maangta hai. Aur social media pe toh sirf highlights hi daalte hain na — woh wali real baat wahan jaati nahi.

Usi ke liye yeh jagah hai.

"Koi bhi topic chota nahi hota — agar woh aapko feel ho raha hai, toh woh matter karta hai."
Yahan aap woh sab share kar sakti hain jo baaki jagah "fit" nahi hota. Aaj ka ek chota sa win jo aapko bahut bada laga — share karo. Ek din jo bohot bura gaya — vent karo. Koi sawaal jo poochne mein sharam aa rahi hai — poochho. Ya bas itna likhna hai ki "hello, main yahan hoon" — woh bhi welcome hai.

Yeh jagah hai
Real baatein karne ke liye. Apne jaisi log dhundhne ke liye. Advice maangne ke liye — bina judge hue. Khud ko thoda halka feel karne ke liye. Aur kabhi kabhi, sirf yeh jaanne ke liye ki aap akeli nahi hain.

Hum sab bahut saari cheezein ek saath carry karti hain — ghar, kaam, rishte, sapne, thakaan. Aur hum mein se bahut saari log itni silently carry karti hain ki baaki sab ko lagta hai sab theek hai. Yahan woh silence tod sakte hain.

✦
Khushi share karo
Choti ho ya badi — celebrate karo. Yahan log genuinely khush honge tumhare liye.

✦
Vent karo, freely
Bura din tha? Koi baat nahi. Yahan sunne wale hain — fix karne ki zaroorat nahi.

✦
Advice maango
Koi bhi sawaal "too small" nahi. Life ke sawaalon ka koi right size nahi hota.

✦
Bas hello bolo
Sirf connect karna hai? Woh bhi kaafi hai. Naye log milte hain yahan.

Ek rule hai yahan — koi judgment nahi. Hum sab apni apni life mein figure it out kar rahi hain. Koi expert nahi, koi perfect nahi. Bas ek doosre ke saath thodi der ke liye honest ho jaana — yeh bhi bahut hota hai.

Shuruwaat karte hain — introduce yourself
01 Aap kahan se ho aur abhi life mein kya chal raha hai?
02 Ek cheez jo aapko genuinely khush karti hai — badi ya choti, kuch bhi.
03 Aap yahan kya dhundh rahi hain — dosti, advice, ya sirf ek jagah exist karne ke liye?
"Aap jaise hain — waise hi welcome hain yahan."

fields.title: Kisne bola tha tumhe chahiye? Ya bas... lagta tha chahiye?
fields.content:
Ek baar seriously socho —
Pehli baar shaadi ka pressure kab feel hua? Aur kahan se aaya?
Koi ek din nahi tha. Koi announcement nahi thi. Bas ek din realize hua ki yeh anxiety — yeh "abhi nahi toh kab" wali feeling — kabse tumhari apni ban gayi?
Woh bani nahi thi. Dali gayi thi.
Kisi ne directly nahi kaha. Par kisi shaadi mein logo ki nazrein tumpe thodi zyada rukne lagi. Ghar mein ek "tere liye rishta aaya tha" wala casual sentence jo itna casual tha hi nahi. Ek birthday jo celebration se zyada deadline lagni lagi. Ek dost ki engagement jiske baad sabne tumhari taraf dekha — congratulations wali nazar se nahi, abhi tak nahi? wali nazar se.
Boond boond. Saal dar saal.
Jab tak tumhe pata chala — woh darr tumhara apna lag raha tha. Woh urgency tumhari apni lag rahi thi. Akele reh jaane ka, peeche reh jaane ka, kuch miss kar jaane ka darr — sab tumhara apna.
Yahi toh conditioning ka kaam hai. Woh tumse permission nahi maangti. Woh seedha tumhare andar nahi aati. Woh itni dheere aati hai ki jab tak settle hoti hai — tumhe lagta hai tum khud yahi chahti thi.
Aur sabse mushkil hissa?
Yeh sab karne wale log tumse pyaar karte hain. Genuinely. Woh protect karna chahte hain tumhe. Unhe bhi yahi sikhaya gaya tha — unhe bhi yahi weight diya gaya tha. Woh bas wahi pass on kar rahe hain jo unhe mila tha.
Par pyaar hone se cheez harmless nahi ho jaati.
Kyunki kahin na kahin, ek sawaal jo sirf tumhara hona chahiye tha —
Kya main yeh chahti hoon? Kya main is insaan ke saath yeh zindagi chahti hoon?
— woh sawaal kabka replace ho chuka tha iss sawaal se:
"Kab karogi? Aur kya wait kar rahi ho?"
Wanting ka step skip hi ho gaya.
Toh iss series mein shaadi, relationships, timelines — sab aayega. Par shuru hoga yahan se. Ek seedha sawaal:
Yeh tumhari genuine wanting hai — ya woh darr hai jo wanting jaisa feel hota hai?
Jawab dena zaroori nahi abhi. Par is sawaal ke saath baitho. Sach mein baitho.
Kyunki yahan se shuru hoti hai tum

fields.title: Ek Sawal - Khudse
fields.content:
Agar aaj — sirf aaj — tumhe koi chai nahi banani hoti. Koi tiffin pack nahi karna hota. Koi "mummy yeh kahan hai" nahi bolta. Koi mood manage nahi karna hota kisi ka.
Tum kya karti?
Ruko. Sochne do.

...

Yaad bhi hai na? Ki tum kya karti thi. Ki tumhe kya pasand tha. Ki tumhara ek alag hi world tha ek time pe.
Ya itna waqt ho gaya hai ki ab woh sawaal hi ajnabi lagta hai?

Yeh ghar ke kaam chote lagte hain bahar se.
Chai. Khana. Bacho ka homework. Sasural ki expectations. Relatives ki opinions. Pati ka mood. Sab ka sab simultaneously.
Aur jab thak jaao — jab ek ghante ki zaroorat ho sirf apne liye — tab sun'ne ko milta hai:
"Aur kya chahiye tujhe? Ghar hai, parivar hai, sab hai." Jaise "sab hai" matlab hai ki tum complete ho. Jaise tumhari existence ka purpose fulfill ho gaya. Jaise tum ek aurat nahi — ek system ho. Jo 24/7 run karta hai. Bina maintenance ke. Bina off-day ke.

Par suno —

Jo aurat bacho ko sambhalte sambhalte ek industry run kar sakti hai.
Jo in-laws ki demands manage karte karte ek country manage kar sakti hai.
Jo sab ka emotional labor uthate uthate mountains hilane ki taakat rakhti hai —
Woh sirf isliye nahi ruk gayi kyunki use "nahi aata."
Woh ruki hai kyunki usse kisi ne bataya hi nahi ki rukna zaroori nahi.
Uski energy — woh sab jo ghar ki chaar deewaaron mein kho jaati hai roz — woh kisi aur cheez pe lagti toh duniya alag hoti.

Toh yeh series usi ke liye hai.
Pehle — khud samajhna ki main kaun hoon in sab roles ke baad bhi.
Phir — dheere dheere — duniya ko ehsaas dilana.
Yahi courage hai. Sabse bada wala.
Apni zindagi wapas maangna — uss ghar mein rehte hue bhi jahan sab samajhte hain ki tumhe aur kya chahiye.
🔥 drop karo agar kabhi tumne bhi yeh sawaal khud se puchha ho — main kaun hoon? Sirf yeh sab ke alawa?

fields.title: Divorce-An End and Also A New Beginning...
fields.content:
Divorce se khatam kya hua?
Ek chapter khatam hua. Aur haan — woh chapter sirf ek relationship nahi tha.
Woh woh subah thi jo tum dono ki thi. Woh plans the jo kabhi bolе nahi gaye par the. Woh aadat thi kisi ki presence ki — chahe woh presence healthy nahi thi tab bhi. Woh future tha jo tumne socha tha — jo shayad kabhi tumhara tha hi nahi, par tumne uske liye khud ko diya tha poora.
Yeh sab khatam hua. Aur yeh dard real hai. Isse chota mat karo. Isse "move on" ke naam pe jaldi mein mat dabaao.

Par ek baat aur bhi sach hai.
Jo tum aaj ho — yeh zero nahi hai.
Zero woh hoti jo kuch seekhi hi nahi. Zero woh hoti jisne khud ko kabhi jaana hi nahi. Zero woh hoti jisne yeh faisla karne ki himmat hi nahi dikhai.
Tum woh nahi ho.
Tum woh ho jisne ek zindagi banayi. Phir dekha ki woh zindagi kaam nahi kar rahi. Phir — aur yeh sabse mushkil hissa hai — usse jaane diya.
Yeh strength hai. Chahe abhi aisa feel na ho.

Kuch din bahut bhaari honge.
Akеlapan aisa hoga jo pehle kabhi feel nahi kiya. Chhoti chhoti cheezein — ek gaana, ek jagah, ek waqt ka khana — achanak bahut kuch yaad dila dengi.
Log opinions denge. Kuch sympathetic honge. Kuch judgmental. Kuch dono ek saath.
Aur tum andar se ek sawaal se lad rahi hogi —
Ab kya?

Ab yeh —
Pehli baar — shayad bahut time baad — tumhari zindagi sirf tumhari hai.
Koi aise future ke liye plan nahi karna jisme tumhara mann nahi tha. Koi aise insaan ko khush karne ki koshish nahi jiske liye tum khud ko kho rahi thi. Koi compromise nahi jo sirf tum kar rahi thi — akele.
Yeh freedom overwhelming lagti hai pehle. Lagti hai — kyunki hoti hai.
Par yeh freedom hai. Real wali.

Starting over ka matlab yeh nahi ki tum wahi banogi jo thi divorce se pehle.
Woh wapas nahi aayega. Aana bhi nahi chahiye.
Tum kuch aur banogi. Koi jo zyada jaanti hai khud ko. Koi jise pata hai ki woh kya nahi chahiye — aur isliye ab clearly dekh sakti hai ki chahiye kya.
Woh foundation hai. Zero nahi.

Yeh series un auraton ke liye hai jinki zindagi mein yeh waqt aaya — ya aa raha hai — ya kisi apni ke liye dekh rahi hain.
Koi judgment nahi yahan.
Sirf yeh — tumhara agla chapter abhi bhi likha jaana baaki hai. Aur is baar — woh tumhare haath mein hai.
🤍 agar yeh tumse baat kar raha hai — ya kisi aisi aurat ke liye feel kiya tumne jo yeh sab jee rahi hai — toh yeh post untak pahuncha do.

fields.title: My Health and Self Care....😂
fields.content:
Apni Health ka dhyan rakhna is not that simple. 

Agar koi kahe Exercise karo. Healthy khao. Itna simple hai. Itna easy hai. Baaki sab toh sambhal leti ho — yeh ek kaam kyon nahi hota? Unhe kya pata ki "yeh ek kaam" kab hota hai aur kab nahi hota.

Yeh woh jagah hai jahan hum aksar khud ko blame karne lagte hain.
Willpower nahi hai. Discipline nahi hai. Seriously liya hi nahi.
Par ek second ke liye ruko.

Ek aurat jo subah uthti hai — ghar sambhalti hai, bacho ko taiyaar karti hai, sabki alag alag demands poori karti hai, sabke liye khana banati hai — aur phir expect kiya jaata hai ki woh apne liye alag se low calorie meal bhi banaye, 45 minute exercise bhi kare, aur yeh sab bina kisi interruption ke kare — yeh "simple" kab se ho gaya?
Kab se ek insaan ke liye itni saari zindagiyan simultaneously jeena — aur upar se khud ke liye bhi waqt aur energy bachana — "itna sa kaam" kehlaane laga?

Sach yeh hai —
Yeh routine isliye nahi ban raha ki tum mein kuch kami hai.
Yeh isliye nahi ban raha kyunki tumhara time, tumhari energy, aur tumhara schedule — yeh sab pehle se hi kisi aur ke naam pe likha hua hai.

Woh log jo "itna sa kaam" kehte hain —
Unka khana koi aur banata hai.
Unke bachon ko koi aur sambhalta hai.
Unka Sunday sach mein sirf unka hota hai.
Isliye unhe yeh sab itna simple lagta hai. Kyunki unki zindagi mein space hai — khud ke liye. Tumhari mein abhi nahi hai.

Apna dhyan rakhna tumhare liye mushkil nahi hai.
Mushkil yeh hai ki tumhari zindagi mein pehle khud ke liye jagah ho — aur yeh kaam koi aur tumhare liye nahi karega.
Yeh woh sach hai jo koi nahi bolta — jab woh apni "exercise karo, healthy khao" wali free salaah dete hain.🤍

Aap mei se jo bhi aisa feel karta hai ki apna dhyan rakhna mushkil hai aur jo bhi apne problems face ki hai isliye liye aap sab kuch yahan share kar sakte hain. We all can listen to each other atleast here.

fields.title: Khudko Pehchano..
fields.content:
Kabhi na kabhi aisa moment aata hai jab tum khud se poochna band kar deti ho — mujhe kya chahiye?
Aur bas yeh sochne lagti ho — logon ko mujhse kya chahiye.

Socho zara…
Woh beti jo hamesha responsible thi.
Woh dost jo hamesha available thi.
Woh ladki jo hamesha “haan” bol deti thi.
Woh aurat jo khud ko thoda chhota kar leti thi taaki dusre comfortable feel karein.

Role pe role…
Aur dheere dheere, kahin beech mein… tum khud se door ho gayi.

Pata bhi nahi chala kab hua.
Koi ek moment nahi tha.
Bas ek slow drift tha…
“I’m fine” bolte bolte.
Adjust karte karte.
Dusron ko samajhne mein itni expert ho gayi…
ki khud ko samajhna hi bhool gayi.

Aur ab kabhi kabhi akele baith ke yeh sawal aata hai —
“Main actually hoon kaun?”

Logon ke liye jo karti ho, uske bina…
Unki expectations ke bina…
Tum kaun ho?

Yeh confusion koi problem nahi hai.
Yeh beginning hai.

Identity ek baar mil jaane wali cheez nahi hoti.
Yeh woh cheez hai jahan tum baar baar wapas aati ho.
Jahan tum unlearn karti ho… aur phir se khud ko build karti ho.

Toh thoda ruk ke socho:

✨ Jab koi nahi dekh raha hota — tum kaun hoti ho?
✨ Tumhe kya pasand hai jo sirf tumhare liye hai — kisi aur ke liye nahi?
✨ Tum kya carry kar rahi ho jo kabhi tumhara tha hi nahi?

Yeh space unhi questions ke liye hai.
Overwhelm hone ke liye nahi…
Balki dheere dheere khud tak wapas aane ke liye.

Kyuki sabse important relationship…
Jo tumhari life mein hai —
Woh tumhara khud ke saath hai.

Aur usse dobara jaanne ke liye… kabhi bhi late nahi hota.

👇
Ek cheez share karo jo tumhe lagta hai truly tumhari hai
— koi value, koi dream, ya tumhari personality ka ek hissa.

💛 Share karo agar kabhi aisa feel kiya ho to

fields.title: “Ek Nayi Zindagi… Aur Ek Nayi Main”
fields.content:
Pregnancy sirf ek phase nahi hota…
yeh ek poori duniya badalne jaisa ehsaas hota hai.

Jab pehli baar pata chalta hai ki tumhare andar ek chhoti si jaan hai,
toh emotions mix ho jaate hain…
thoda darr, thodi excitement, aur ek ajeeb si khushi jo words mein explain nahi hoti.

Kabhi bina wajah aansu aa jaate hain,
kabhi chhoti si baat pe hasi rukti hi nahi…
Log kehte hain hormones —
par sach toh yeh hai ki dil dheere dheere maa ban raha hota hai.

Raat ko neend kam ho jaati hai…
par sapne badh jaate hain.
Us chhoti si muskaan ke,
uski chhoti si ungli pakadne ke,
usse pehli baar “mumma” sunne ke…

Aur phir ek din…
jab tum usse apni baahon mein pakadti ho,
toh sab kuch ek second ke liye ruk sa jaata hai.

Tab samajh aata hai —
tum sirf ek baby ko janam nahi deti…
tum ek nayi khud ko janam deti ho.

Motherhood perfect nahi hota.
Kabhi thakaan hoti hai, kabhi confusion, kabhi self-doubt bhi…
par sach yeh hai —
yeh journey jitni real hai, utni hi khoobsurat bhi hai ❤️

👇
Agar aap maa hain ya banne wali hain —
ek feeling share karo jo aapne is journey mein deeply feel ki ho.
